job_description:
Sr. B2B Content Specialist

Company Overview 2Base Technologies is a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, serving mid-market and enterprise clients in the US and UK. Our expertise spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernization, and operational transformation.

Role Purpose This senior writing role owns the written voice of the marketing function end-to-end. It is not a content management or social media role. The position operationalizes, applies, and enforces the company’s voice across all written output, ensuring technical credibility, commercial relevance, and a clear point of view.

Key Responsibilities - Own all long-form content (pillar pages, blogs, playbooks, decision frameworks, use-case libraries) targeting senior decision-makers in the US and UK - Write content in the voice of senior leadership for professional publishing - Audit and enforce the 2Base content voice across all published material - Create clear content briefs executable by junior team members - Act as the content quality gatekeeper for all published work - Leverage AI tools as a productivity layer, not a substitute for thinking

What You Will Not Own - SEO keyword selection (managed by the SEO Specialist) - Social media scheduling (managed by a separate team member) - Visual design (managed by the Visual Designer) - Distribution strategy (managed by the Marketing & Growth Lead) - ICP definition (you write for the defined ICP)

Requirements - Minimum 3-5 years of B2B content writing experience (strong candidates have 5-6 years) - Proven ability to rank content on Google - Experience writing in voices other than your own (executives, founders, senior professionals) - Working-level SEO understanding (search intent, content alignment) - Experience with AI writing tools and their limitations - Demonstrable content contributions to measurable outcomes (traffic, leads, engagement) - Background in technology, SaaS, or professional services preferred

Writing Ability - Write with a clear point of view, not neutral summaries - Explain complex technical concepts in plain language - Write concisely without filler or unnecessary qualification - Adapt to multiple authentic voices

Mindset - View content as a commercial tool, not a creative exercise - Comfortable with editing and feedback - Curious about AI, technology, and business operations - Fast-paced team environment

What We Are Not Looking For - Portfolios of generic "Top 10 Tips" or trend summaries - Writers who cannot demonstrate content performance metrics - Candidates without experience writing for technical/senior audiences - Writers who only produce self-directed content or cannot adapt to briefs - Candidates who treat AI tools as primary authors

Benefits - Growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill

Contact If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, share your updated resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com
summary: Senior B2B Content Specialist required to own and enforce 2Base Technologies' written voice across technical and business leadership audiences, with 3-5 years of proven B2B content experience and SEO expertise.

job_description:
Organic Growth & SEO Specialist

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from a clear brief, and this role owns the organic search engine that sits at the centre of it.

About Us We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do This role does not optimise content after it is written. It determines what gets written, why, and how it is structured so that the right people find it at the right moment in their decision process.

The organic search function at 2Base is being built to generate qualified inbound pipeline from two specific markets, the US and UK, at scale. That means a large volume of high-intent pages, a technical foundation that performs, and a programmatic architecture that compounds over time. The person who fills this role designs that system and owns its results.

This is a strategic and technical role. It is not an SEO support role, a blogging role with keyword research added on, or a task-execution position within a larger team.

What You Will Own - The organic traffic strategy for the US and UK markets, including keyword targeting and the full architecture of how search drives inbound pipeline - Keyword research and search intent mapping across all service lines and target audiences - Page architecture of the website, including what pages exist, what they target, how they are structured, and how they connect through internal linking - On-page SEO across all published pages - Technical SEO oversight, working directly with the development team on crawlability, indexing, site speed, Core Web Vitals, and structured data - Google Search Console as a working instrument, including weekly review, issue resolution, and opportunity identification - The programmatic SEO build, including designing the framework and templates that allow the site to scale to 80 to 120+ high-intent pages - SEO briefs written clearly enough that the Content Specialist can execute without guessing - Link building target approval. The outreach team identifies candidates, and you approve or reject based on defined quality standards - SEO performance reporting, including organic traffic, keyword movement, pages published, and Search Console health

What You Will Not Own - Writing content: you brief the Content Specialist, you do not write the pages - Link building outreach: a dedicated team member executes this, you set the standard - Paid search: that is a separate role - Social media distribution: separate channel, separate owner - Content calendar scheduling: the Marketing & Growth Lead sets distribution priorities

Requirements - Experience: Minimum 3 to 5 years in an SEO or organic growth role, with strong candidates typically at the 4 to 6 year mark - Experience ranking high-intent commercial pages, not just informational content - Experience managing technical SEO independently - Experience building or contributing to a programmatic SEO architecture - Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console as an active tool - Experience working across US and/or UK markets - Working knowledge of AEO, GEO, and AIO - Ability to write SEO briefs that a content writer can execute without needing clarification

Mindset - Thinks in systems, not tasks - Comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage environments - Analytically honest - Collaborative but not passive

What We Are Not Looking For - SEO practitioners whose entire experience is informational content - Candidates who treat Search Console as a monthly reporting task - Candidates who cannot explain the difference between high-intent and informational keyword targeting - Candidates whose SEO process depends entirely on tools - Candidates with no exposure to US or UK search behaviour

Benefits - A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Strategic SEO role focused on building scalable organic growth systems for US/UK markets. Requires 3-5 years of experience in high-intent SEO and technical execution.

job_description:
Sr. Visual Designer 2Base Technologies

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from scratch, and this role makes it look like a company worth taking seriously. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

Before a CTO reads a word of our content, they see the design. Before they trust the argument, they have already made a judgment about whether this company looks credible. This role owns that first impression, and then owns every asset that follows.

The visual function at 2Base is being built from the ground up. There is no brand system to inherit, no template library to use, and no consistent visual identity to maintain. What exists is a clear strategic direction, a defined audience of senior technology and operations leaders, and a marketing team building the content engine around it. Your job is to give that engine a visual identity it deserves.

The first 30 days of this role have one priority: build the brand system. Everything else, including LinkedIn carousels, ad creatives, landing pages, and presentation templates, is built from that foundation. A designer who wants to jump straight into production before the system exists is not the right fit.

This is not a creative agency role. The audience is sceptical, senior, and technically literate. Design that looks clever but reads as unclear, or feels consumer-facing rather than professional, will not work with this ICP. Restraint, precision, and consistency matter more than visual novelty.

What You Will Own

  • The 2Base visual brand system, including colour palette, typography, spacing, layout rules, and the documentation that makes it usable by the whole team
  • All marketing and content visual assets, including LinkedIn carousels, single-image posts, social graphics, infographics, and diagrams
  • Paid advertising creatives, including Google Display and LinkedIn Ads, designed to be tested and iterated
  • Landing page visual design, including layout, components, and conversion-focused structure, handed to the development team for build
  • Email visual templates, presentation templates, and sales deck templates
  • A template library that enables other team members to produce on-brand assets independently
  • Design consistency auditing, including periodic review of all published assets to ensure alignment with the brand system

Secondary Responsibility

  • Light video editing and motion for social reels, YouTube, and website use, supporting other team members

What You Will Not Own

  • What an asset communicates. The Content and Thought Leadership Specialist owns the message, argument, and information hierarchy. You may suggest improvements in structure, but content decisions remain with them
  • Copywriting. The Content Specialist writes the words; you structure them visually
  • Website development. You design the pages; the development team builds them
  • Content strategy. You execute what is briefed, not decide what gets created
  • Brand positioning. The Marketing Operating System defines how 2Base communicates; you interpret it visually

Requirements

Experience

  • Minimum 3 to 5 years in a B2B design role, preferably in technology, SaaS, or professional services
  • Experience building a brand system or significantly evolving one, not just producing isolated assets
  • Experience designing for LinkedIn, including carousel formats, document posts, and single-image feed content
  • Experience creating paid ad creatives that have been executed, tested, and iterated
  • Experience designing landing pages with an understanding of conversion principles
  • Comfort with basic video editing for social and web content, including reels, short-form YouTube, and simple website assets
  • Proficiency in Figma (non-negotiable), and Adobe Creative Suite including Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and After Effects

Design Thinking

  • Portfolio demonstrates restraint through clean, structured, and purposeful design
  • Ability to explain the reasoning behind key design decisions
  • Designs with a specific audience in mind, not for general appeal
  • Strong understanding and consistent application of typography as a functional tool
  • Ability to produce work that appears credible to a senior B2B audience at first glance

Mindset

  • Able to work effectively from structured briefs
  • Works at speed without compromising quality
  • Treats design as a communication tool rather than purely creative expression
  • Open to feedback and able to give it constructively

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Portfolios dominated by consumer brands, lifestyle work, event design, or campaigns with no B2B relevance
  • Designers who can describe visuals but cannot justify their decisions
  • Candidates without experience building a brand system
  • Designers who overcomplicate visuals with excessive fonts, colours, styles, or effects
  • Candidates who require complete creative freedom and struggle with structured briefs

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Build a professional visual brand system for a tech company targeting senior B2B audiences, creating marketing assets and ensuring design consistency.

job_description:

  • Own the testing lifecycle: planning, documentation, execution, defect management, and release validation.
  • Conduct manual and automated testing for web and mobile applications to ensure stability and reliability.
  • Develop and maintain test cases, test data, execution reports, and quality validation documentation.

  • Skills:

  • Responsive/cross-browser testing
  • Software testing methodologies and QA processes

job_description:
QA Engineer

We are seeking a detail-oriented Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years of hands-on experience in software testing to join our QA team. In this role, you will ensure the quality and reliability of web and mobile applications by participating in test planning, test case creation, test execution, and defect tracking. You will collaborate with developers, project teams, and senior QA engineers to understand requirements, identify risks, and validate application functionality across scenarios. This role demands a strong testing mindset, attention to detail, and the ability to identify edge cases and potential product issues.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Review BRD, SRS, user stories, and functional specifications to understand system requirements.
  • Translate requirements into structured test scenarios and documented test cases for comprehensive coverage.
  • Perform functional, regression, and exploratory testing for web and mobile applications across browsers, devices, and environments.
  • Identify edge cases, alternate flows, and boundary conditions to validate system behavior beyond standard use cases.
  • Log, document, and track defects with clear reproduction steps, evidence, and severity classification using defect management tools.
  • Validate bug fixes and perform regression testing to ensure stability during releases.
  • Maintain test documentation, execution reports, and QA artifacts for traceability and quality tracking.
  • Support test summaries, progress updates, and QA reports during development and release cycles.
  • Collaborate with business analysts, developers, project managers, and senior QA engineers to clarify requirements and resolve issues.
  • Participate in Agile/Scrum ceremonies (sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews).
  • Perform basic API validation and data verification for correct system behavior at the integration level.
  • Own assigned modules or features, ensuring testing completeness and readiness for release.
  • Contribute to improving test coverage, testing approaches, and QA processes.
  • Test AI-powered/data-driven features by validating output accuracy, response consistency, and system behavior.
  • Learn automation fundamentals, structured testing techniques, and emerging QA tools, including AI testing practices.

Required Skills & Mindset:

  • Strong attention to detail and structured thinking.
  • Willingness to understand business logic before testing.
  • Ability to identify edge cases and risks beyond happy paths.
  • Analytical mindset to question unexpected system behavior.
  • Clear written and verbal communication for collaboration.
  • Strong documentation skills for defect reporting and test case writing.
  • Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.
  • Solid understanding of functional, regression, integration, and exploratory testing.
  • Experience with test case design techniques.
  • Familiarity with bug tracking and test management tools.
  • Basic exposure to API testing (Postman, REST Assured).
  • Understanding of Agile/Scrum methodologies and QA’s role in sprint cycles.
  • Familiarity with AI-assisted testing.

Good to Have:

  • Basic awareness of performance and security testing.
  • Interest in learning automation frameworks (Selenium, Playwright, Cypress).
  • Scripting knowledge in Python, JavaScript, or Java.
  • Exposure to AI-enabled applications (chatbots, recommendation engines) and testing aspects like prompt variations and edge case validation.
  • Awareness of modern QA practices (continuous testing in CI/CD pipelines).
  • Ability to use AI-assisted tools for test case generation, test data preparation, and productivity improvements.
  • Curiosity about test data management (synthetic/test data generation).
  • Experience testing mobile applications or cross-platform systems, or openness to learning mobile testing frameworks (Appium).
  • Risk-based testing and prioritization mindset.
  • Commitment to continuous learning in QA tools, methodologies, and industry practices.
  • ISTQB certification or equivalent testing certification.

Experience & Education:

  • 1–3 years of experience.
  • B.Tech in IT, CS, or MCA (Graduates).

Contact: Share your resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: An Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years of experience in manual testing, test case design, defect tracking, and collaboration with cross-functional teams to ensure product quality in web and mobile applications.

job_description:
Sr. Business Analyst

At 2Base, we are rebuilding the Business Analyst function into a world-class, product-minded, clarity-driven team. This role is not a documentation role. It is not a screen updater role. It is not a support role. This is a core Business Analyst position responsible for functional modelling, system reasoning, requirement clarity, and building the foundation for correct development.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead requirement workshops, client calls, and discovery discussions to understand business needs clearly.
  • Ask deeper questions, challenge assumptions, and validate the true business intent behind every requirement.
  • Convert ambiguous client inputs into structured, logical, and actionable functional understanding.
  • Break large features into modules, workflows, components, and clear dependencies.
  • Build strong system-level understanding involving APIs, integrations, data flow, and backend considerations.
  • Translate business processes into functional logic that development teams can execute.
  • Decompose requirements into use-case flows, system behaviours, edge cases, alternate paths, validation rules, and dependencies.
  • Prepare high-quality documentation, including BRD, FRD, SRS, workflows, state diagrams, data-flow diagrams, API requirement specs, and acceptance criteria.
  • Ensure all documentation is clear, logical, structured, testable, and ready for developers and QA to use.
  • Collaborate closely with developers, architects, QA, and product owners to validate feasibility and align understanding.
  • Ensure QA teams can derive test cases effectively from your functional logic and acceptance criteria.
  • Communicate confidently with clients, handle requirement discussions smoothly, and clarify misunderstandings immediately.
  • Push back politely on unclear or changing requirements and ensure final decisions are documented properly.
  • Maintain requirement traceability throughout the lifecycle and ensure zero ambiguity during handover to development.
  • Own the entire requirement lifecycle from discovery to development readiness with high accountability.
  • Drive clarity across teams by ensuring every requirement is complete, validated, and logically consistent.

Required Skills & Experience

  • Minimum 4+ years of core Business Analyst experience with strong analytical and system-level thinking.
  • Ability to break down complex requirements into clear components, workflows, and functional logic.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication with confidence in client interactions and clarifications.
  • Proven experience in preparing SRS, BRD, FRD, workflows, and acceptance criteria with high clarity.
  • Ability to challenge assumptions politely, ask the right questions, and ensure zero ambiguity.
  • Strong collaboration experience with development, QA, architects, and cross-functional teams.
  • Understanding of API behaviours, integrations, data flows, and multi-module system interactions.
  • Ability to run requirement discussions, workshops, and independently lead discovery sessions.
  • Exposure to SaaS, scalable products, automation, or AI-supported systems is an added advantage.
  • Demonstrated ownership of requirements end-to-end, ensuring developer-ready and testable outputs.
  • Not suitable for UI-only thinkers, passive communicators, template-based writers, or those relying on AI for answers.
  • Success is defined by clear, testable requirements, zero rework, strong cross-team alignment, and confident communication.
  • Candidates must align with 2Base’s structured thinking, clarity-driven culture, and transformation of the BA function.

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, or related field
  • 4 to 8 years of experience as a Business Analyst or Product Analyst
  • Experience in SaaS or scalable product environments
  • Exposure to AI/Automation, integrations, or multi-module systems
  • Ability to run independent client workshops
  • Must have independently handled requirement ownership for at least one full project lifecycle

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.

job_description:
Senior DevSecOps Engineer

We are seeking a Senior DevSecOps Engineer to lead cybersecurity, infrastructure automation, and DevSecOps practices as we transition to a secure, scalable, and modern engineering environment. This role will strengthen 2Base’s security posture, modernize pipelines, and guide the team toward a secure-by-design culture. You will collaborate with engineering, product, and leadership teams to ensure systems, applications, and cloud environments are secure, reliable, and future-ready.

Key Responsibilities

  • Define, implement, and enforce security standards, policies, and best practices across cloud and on-prem environments.
  • Conduct security risk assessments, vulnerability scans, penetration testing, and execute mitigation plans.
  • Architect, maintain, and enhance secure CI/CD pipelines with automated compliance and governance checkpoints.
  • Promote a "Security First" culture across engineering, ensuring secure design and development practices.
  • Monitor, detect, investigate, and respond to security threats, incidents, breaches, and anomalies.
  • Develop Infrastructure-as-Code practices using Terraform, Ansible, and automation frameworks.
  • Design secure cloud architectures across AWS, Azure, or GCP for scalable, compliant deployments.
  • Build and secure containerized environments using Docker and Kubernetes with enforced security baselines.
  • Implement secure monitoring, logging, SIEM, and alerting systems for infrastructure and application layers.
  • Optimize system reliability, scalability, performance, and fault tolerance for critical workloads.
  • Mentor DevOps engineers in secure development, automation, and operational excellence.
  • Lead technical strategy, tool selection, process improvements, and DevSecOps roadmap execution.
  • Collaborate with BA, PM, and Engineering teams to align security, performance, and delivery goals.

Required Skills & Experience

  • 4–8 years of experience in DevOps, Cloud, or Infrastructure engineering with strong security exposure.
  • Hands-on experience with CI/CD tools like Jenkins, GitLab CI, or GitHub Actions.
  • Expertise in AWS, Azure, or GCP cloud platforms with secure architecture focus.

Execution Agility & Task Ownership - Take ownership of ambiguous, loosely defined tasks and independently drive them to completion. - Deliver any required artefact (functional, operational, analytical, or presentation) based on client needs. - Operate beyond traditional BA scope when required.

AI-Augmented Workflow Design - Use Claude and other AI tools to prototype solution concepts, workflow designs, and process models — enabling rapid stakeholder feedback cycles. - Identify and document high-value automation and AI-augmentation opportunities within existing business processes. - Work with AI engineering teams to define how LLM, RAG, and agentic features interact with user journeys and business workflows. - Contribute to prompt design reviews and AI output evaluation sessions — providing the business context that engineers need to tune AI behaviour. - Build reusable AI-assisted BA templates: story maps, discovery frameworks, workshop canvases, and output evaluation checklists.

Stakeholder & Client Engagement - Act as the primary bridge between business stakeholders, clients, and AI engineering teams throughout the delivery lifecycle. - Prepare and present business cases, solution options, and progress updates to senior stakeholders and C-suite audiences. - Produce polished MOM documents, decision logs, RAID registers, and client-facing status communications — augmented with AI tooling for speed and quality. - Manage stakeholder expectations proactively — surfacing risks, scope changes, and dependency blockers early.

Agile Delivery & Programme Support - Own and maintain a well-groomed product backlog — writing, prioritising, and refining stories with the Tech Lead and Product Manager. - Lead sprint ceremonies: planning, reviews, retrospectives, and backlog refinement for AI product squads. - Support UAT planning and execution — defining test scenarios, coordinating business testers, and managing feature sign-off. - Track and report delivery progress, velocity, and risks across sprints and programme increments.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

Experience & Education - 8+ years of business analysis experience on technology-led programmes, with consistent delivery in Agile environments. - 2+ years of active, hands-on use of AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Notion AI, or equivalent) in professional BA workflows — not just awareness. - Proven experience engaging with senior stakeholders including C-suite, programme directors, and enterprise clients. - Direct involvement in at least one AI, LLM, or data-driven product delivery — requirements, UAT, or workflow design. - Bachelor's degree in Business, Computer Science, Information Systems, or equivalent; BA certification (CBAP, CCBA, BCS Diploma) preferred.

Core BA Skills - Expert-level requirements elicitation, analysis, and documentation across waterfall, Agile, and hybrid delivery models. - Strong process mapping and modelling skills: BPMN, swimlane diagrams, user journey maps, and value stream mapping. - Skilled in writing precise, unambiguous user stories and acceptance criteria that engineering and QA teams can execute against without rework. - Excellent facilitation skills for discovery workshops, design sprints, and sprint ceremonies. - Proficiency with BA tooling: Jira, Confluence, Miro, Lucidchart, MS Visio, and equivalent platforms.

AI Tool Proficiency - Claude (Anthropic): daily use for drafting, prototyping, summarising, and structuring BA artefacts and communications. - ChatGPT / GPT-4o: supplementary LLM for ideation, content drafting, and quick analysis. - Notion AI / Confluence AI: intelligent documentation, auto-summarisation, and knowledge management. - Miro AI / FigJam: AI-assisted process and journey mapping, rapid wireframing, and workshop facilitation. - Jira AI plugins or equivalent: story generation, backlog triage, and sprint reporting assistance. - Comfortable evaluating AI output quality — spotting hallucinations, gaps, and areas needing human refinement.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • Experience in fintech, payments, or regulated environments — familiarity with PCI-DSS, compliance, data residency, and audit requirements.
  • Exposure to dispute management, chargeback processing, or payments operations — highly advantageous.
  • Experience producing CPO- or CTO-facing strategic documents, business cases, or executive briefing packs using AI-assisted drafting.
  • Familiarity with responsible AI principles: bias, explainability, data privacy, and ethical use guidelines.
  • Experience working in a Global Delivery or offshore-onshore model with distributed teams across time zones.

CORE COMPETENCIES

🔍 Master Elicitor: Draws out real requirements from even the most ambiguous stakeholder conversations — structured, complete, and unambiguous. 🤖 AI Power User: Uses Claude, ChatGPT, and AI tooling fluently to draft, prototype, summarise, and validate BA artefacts faster and better. 🗺️ Process Thinker: Maps current and future-state processes with clarity — identifying gaps, inefficiencies, and AI automation opportunities. 🤝 Stakeholder Fluency: Engages confidently across all levels — from end users and SMEs to C-suite and enterprise clients. ⚡ Rapid Prototyper: Uses AI tools to prototype workflows, wireframes, and solution concepts quickly — enabling faster stakeholder validation. 🛡️ Risk & Compliance Lens: Spots regulatory, data privacy, and auditability risks early — essential in fintech and regulated environments.
